transformation_type: annulation of acylmethylpyridinium salts with alpha,beta-unsaturated ketones and ammonia equivalents to substituted pyridines
mechanism_type: Michael addition of pyridinium-derived nucleophiles to enones followed by cyclization/dehydration of 1,5-diketone-type intermediates
reactant_pattern_text: alpha-haloketone + pyridine to acylmethylpyridinium salt + alpha,beta-unsaturated ketone + NH3 equivalent
common_conditions: ammonium acetate or related ammonia equivalent, often heated in acetic acid or alcoholic solvent
description_short: Pyridine-annulation method that converts acylmethylpyridinium salts and enones into substituted pyridines through Michael addition, diketone formation, and cyclodehydration.

transformation_type: titanium-mediated conversion of esters or amides into cyclopropanols and cyclopropylamines with Grignard reagents
mechanism_type: formation of titanacyclopropane from Ti(O-i-Pr)4 and Grignard reagent followed by dialkylation/cyclopropanation of carboxylic acid derivatives
reactant_pattern_text: ester or amide + Grignard reagent + Ti(O-i-Pr)4
common_conditions: low temperature to room temperature with titanium alkoxide and excess Grignard reagent, followed by acidic workup
description_short: Titanium-mediated cyclopropanation of esters and amides that furnishes cyclopropanols or cyclopropylamines via titanacyclopropane intermediates.

transformation_type: Ni- or Pd-catalyzed cross-coupling of aryl or alkenyl halides with Grignard or organolithium reagents
mechanism_type: oxidative addition, transmetalation, and reductive elimination at Ni or Pd catalysts
reactant_pattern_text: aryl or alkenyl halide + Grignard reagent or organolithium reagent
common_conditions: Ni or Pd catalyst with organomagnesium reagent, often room temperature to reflux depending on substrate class
description_short: Transition-metal-catalyzed cross-coupling that joins aryl or alkenyl halides with organomagnesium or related organometallic reagents to form C-C bonds.

transformation_type: Pd-catalyzed heteroannulation of o-iodoanilines with internal alkynes to indoles
mechanism_type: oxidative addition to o-iodoaniline followed by alkyne carbopalladation, C-N bond formation, and reductive elimination
common_conditions: Pd catalyst, chloride additive, base, and internal alkyne under heating
description_short: Palladium-catalyzed annulation of o-iodoanilines with alkynes that provides substituted indoles and fused azaindole-type scaffolds with predictable regioselectivity.

transformation_type: TPAP/NMO oxidation of primary and secondary alcohols to aldehydes and ketones
mechanism_type: catalytic ruthenate oxidation of alcohols with NMO or related co-oxidants under mild nonaqueous conditions
common_conditions: room temperature to mild cooling with catalytic TPAP, NMO, and molecular sieves in dry solvent
description_short: Mild ruthenate-catalyzed alcohol oxidation that converts sensitive primary and secondary alcohols into aldehydes and ketones using TPAP and NMO.
